full_prompt: Design the 3D-printed monocoque car body for a schools-class CO2-cartridge scale model racecar benchmark. Overall vehicle length must be between 170 mm and 210 mm; width between 50 mm and 85 mm excluding wings; total height (including wings) between 40 mm and 65 mm. The car is propelled by a single 8 g CO2 cartridge that fires into a fully-enclosed driver-side cylindrical chamber of internal diameter 5.5 mm and depth 50 mm, located on the car centerline. The vehicle runs on a 20 m straight track suspended from a tensioned nylon fishing line routed through two 3.5 mm diameter guide tubes on the underside of the car. Material is a single 3D-printed polymer, permitted: PLA, PETG, or ASA (use PLA for the baseline model with Young's modulus 3.5 GPa, Poisson 0.36, tensile yield 55 MPa, ultimate 65 MPa, density 1240 kg/m^3). The body includes front and rear wings with minimum chord 15 mm, maximum chord 30 mm, minimum thickness 2.5 mm, and maximum thickness 8 mm. All decals must be flush within 2 mm of the surface. Load cases: (LC1) CO2 impulse: at trigger, the 8 g CO2 cartridge releases approximately 12 g (0.012 kg) of gas at an initial pressure of ~6 MPa through a 3 mm throat generating a thrust peak of ~70 N for ~10 ms, applied axially at the cartridge throat against the forward chamber wall; (LC2) guide line tension: total aerodynamic + friction drag of ~2 N acts upward through the two guide tubes during the 20 m run; (LC3) handling drop: 1 m free-fall of the fully assembled car (mass 50 g) onto a flat surface landing on one rear wheel, modeled as 20 g impulse at the rear-right wheel hub; (LC4) finish-line capture: deceleration of ~25 g over 10 ms at the front nose as the car impacts the foam capture block. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises stress under LC1-LC4 must remain below 37 MPa (yield 55 / 1.5), (R2) chamber-wall radial expansion under LC1 must remain below 0.1 mm to avoid cartridge seating failure, (R3) car total mass must remain above 50 g (minimum regulation) and below 120 g, (R4) first natural body-bending mode must exceed 200 Hz to avoid coupling with track vibration. Deliverables: STEP AP242, STL, FEA per case, modal report, mass sheet, and a wing span/chord/thickness measurement table certified against the rulebook.
short_prompt:
Design the 3D-printed monocoque body of a schools-class CO2-cartridge scale racecar running on a 20 m tensioned-line track. Material class: a single 3D-printed polymer (PLA baseline). No external structural standard is cited; design to a 1.5 FoS on yield.

Envelope: length 170-210 mm, width 50-85 mm (ex wings), height 40-65 mm (incl wings); cylindrical CO2 chamber 5.5 mm ID x 50 mm deep on centerline; two 3.5 mm guide tubes; wing chord 15-30 mm, thickness 2.5-8 mm; total mass 50-120 g.

Load cases.
- LC1 CO2 impulse: 8 g cartridge gives ~70 N peak axial thrust for ~10 ms at the forward chamber wall.
- LC2 guide-line drag: ~2 N upward through the two guide tubes during the run.
- LC3 drop: 1 m free-fall onto one rear wheel, 20 g impulse at the rear-right hub, ~50 g car mass.
- LC4 finish-line capture: ~25 g deceleration over 10 ms at the nose.

prompt.title: Schools-class 3DP scale racecar body
prompt.brief: 3D-printed monocoque scale car with 8 g CO2 cartridge propulsion on a 20 m track.
